frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: value updating via simulating an internal model of the environment

frameworks.name: imagination-augmented RL with optimism bonus
frameworks.one_line: RL model extended with a bonus added onto imagined rewards

core_question: How does imaginative simulation of paths bias subsequent value-based decisions, and what neural systems support this?
key_assumptions: agents can update value estimates by simulating an internal world model
accuracy of the internal model gates rational imagination-based learning
imagined rewards enter the same value computation as experienced rewards
manipulations: whether participants imagined a path before sequential decisions
presence vs absence of outcome feedback along imagined paths
individual differences in optimism about imagined rewards
key_findings: imagination biases participants toward imagined paths even when suboptimal
the bias scales with participants' optimism about imagined-path rewards
feedback strongly attenuates the imagination-induced bias
valuation network activity predicts the magnitude of the imagination effect
interpretations: imagination supports learning but is susceptible to motivational/optimism biases
an optimism bonus on imagined rewards captures the behavioural effect within RL
